source: pred.gg public profile + own aggregate baselines

archetype.label: The True Flex
archetype.receipt: 5 roles at 100+ games, all within 2.2 wins per 100 of each other — genuinely fill-proof.

entries.change: Queue offlane instead of your average mix
entries.receipt: 54.1% there vs 52.7% overall, 152 games of evidence

ledger.note: wins per 100 games spent on each change; entries overlap and do not sum

plan: Queue offlane as primary. It is your best role at 54.1% over 152 games (your overall is 52.7%). Set support as secondary, not the other way around.
Two-hero rule for the climb: Murdock and Narbash. Compared with the average player on the same hero, you win about 8.8 and 2.5 more games per 100 on them.
Your champion pool is 24+ heroes wide and your top three are only 30% of your games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on your top three.
Park Gadget, Lt. Belica for ranked: 43% over 42; 41% over 34. Keep them for normals.
The math: you are at 824 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. Your all-time peak is 855.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward your own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where you win, not why

insights.title: Narbash is the long-term relationship
insights.finding: 205 games and you genuinely out-pilot the field on Narbash — about 2.5 more wins per 100 games than the average Narbash player. The hours show.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 13 pentakills on record and a 32-kill spree. Mr_Meat31 has receipts.

insights.title: Willy brings out your best
insights.finding: Your record alongside Willy Guggenheim is 54% over 291 ranked games — your strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of you queue, make it this one.

insights.title: Your lean, relative to the squad: vision work
insights.finding: Mr_Meat31's profile sits mid-pack on most axes; the clearest lean is vision work at 0.8x the squad average. Steady is a style too — the lineup can build around predictability.
